name: rad-explain
description: Honest project explanation for any repo. Five skills produce audience-targeted external communications from a project's internal artifacts (code, docs, planning files): `narrate-project` (plain-English description, audience-adaptive — non-dev / investor / future-self / new-collaborator); `elevator-pitch` (~150 words / ~30 seconds spoken); `draft-pitch` (one-pager / executive summary / deck-equivalent for funding, grants, partnerships); `explain-document` (interpret a specific repo file — "what does this commit me to?", "what does this assume?", "where could this go wrong?"); `ground-readme` (generate or audit a README / marketplace listing so it doesn't overpromise). Two pure-stdlib Python validators run on every output: `check-grounding.py` (every substantive claim must trace to repo source — flag unbacked claims) and `check-overpromise.py` (flag superlatives, vague-quantity claims without enumeration, marketing fluff, hype words). Works on any repo with no rad-planner dependency — reads `docs/` if present (vision.md, decisions/, planning/current.md, status.md, roadmap.md), falls back to README + package.json + source structure when absent. Pairs with rad-planner's `/project-story` for canonical-doc-set narratives. The principle: every claim should be checkable against source; the alternative is marketing-shaped content that strangers can't verify. Eats own dog food on the marketplace's "no sensational copy" guardrail.
